trevor-bachmeyer.acute_vs_chronic_note: Dr Bachmeyer often distinguishes acute (injury/infection) vs chronic (longevity/maintenance) dosing. Acute_signals + chronic_signals fields surface candidate contexts; full distillation pending Codex pass.

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider Hunter's MCAS-stack framing as the operative voice on PEA: Hunter (email threadId 19ca15c5dc80c2e0, 2026-02-27): "PEA (Palmitoylethanolamide) — CLEAN MAST CELL STABILIZER" and "Not a peptide — but extremely important" with a loading-then-maintenance schedule. We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh the gut-driven IBS use case Hunter (email threadId 19506a8ec76bb2df, 2025-02-14) lists — "Best peptides for gut health/IBS? BPC-157, TB-500, KPV, oral tributyrin, PEA, and larazotide" — and to confirm the ultra-micronized form, since Hunter (email threadId 19ca15c5dc80c2e0, 2026-02-27) explicitly says "Use ultra-micronized form for absorption." Taylor mentions PEA only in passing as a component of oral gut-repair blends and gives no PEA-specific dose; Trevor's transcripts contain no Palmitoylethanolamide mentions (all keyword hits are 'pea protein' / 'pea smoothie' food references), so the librarian record is single-expert.
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- PEA is NOT a peptide — Hunter (email threadId 19ca15c5dc80c2e0, 2026-02-27): "Not a peptide — but extremely important."
- Form matters — Hunter (email threadId 19ca15c5dc80c2e0, 2026-02-27): "Use ultra-micronized form for absorption."
- Long-term continuation flagged — Hunter (email threadId 19ca15c5dc80c2e0, 2026-02-27): "Long-term - LDN + PEA often continued long term."
- Hunter's broader MCAS-stack honest caveat applies — Hunter (email threadId 19ca15c5dc80c2e0, 2026-02-27): "NOT a cure-all" and "Genetic / mold-driven / trauma-driven nervous system / complex autoimmune cases — no stack fixes everything."
- Single-expert evidence base — Trevor's transcript hits are all food-pea (e.g., Trevor (qd3wIAKuaME 00:00:30.240): "every wannabe soy pea protein"; Trevor (5O4vqJO61QU 00:26:43.679): "Got to get this really great pea protein"; Trevor (PEbu7E8QB_Y 00:27:45.120): "Or whatever else like pea protein"); no Trevor doses or claims about Palmitoylethanolamide are on record.
- Taylor's PEA mentions are blend-level only — Taylor (hgI5Mpc-xDQ 00:15:40.160): "and pea in them and those are also really good to help with inflammation"; Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:57:35.040): "you got BPC KPV PEA some of those other things in there" — Taylor never gives a PEA-specific dose, so practitioner-level dosing must come from Hunter's verbatim or external authority.
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: Where Hunter and Taylor overlap they both treat PEA as part of an oral gut/inflammation-modulating stack rather than a standalone protocol. Hunter (email threadId 19506a8ec76bb2df, 2025-02-14): "Best peptides for gut health/IBS? BPC-157, TB-500, KPV, oral tributyrin, PEA, and larazotide." Taylor (hgI5Mpc-xDQ 00:15:37.600): "most of those gut repair formulas have like tributinrin and pea in them and those are also really good to help with inflammation." Taylor (JC1poI1OEog 00:57:35.040): "one of those peptide oral blends too with larazotide in it, but you got BPC KPV PEA some of those other things in there too." Trevor's transcripts contain no on-topic PEA quotes to confirm or refute.
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: There is no on-record disagreement, because only Hunter speaks to PEA dose, mechanism, and protocol. Taylor never gives a PEA-specific dose (only blend-level mentions), and Trevor's keyword hits are all food-pea protein references — for example Trevor (HbgvbVP8B-k 00:48:33.359): "rice protein and your pea protein and your little vegan proteins, knock it off" and Trevor (G6QnofqaYfM 00:14:22.480): "unless you're eating guava and pea protein." Practitioners should note this asymmetry: any PEA-dose claim in this card is single-source Hunter; if Taylor or Trevor publish PEA-specific guidance later it should be added and reconciled.

acute.dose: Hunter (email threadId 19ca15c5dc80c2e0, 2026-02-27): "Loading: 600 mg twice daily, 4-8 weeks."
acute.route: Hunter (email threadId 19ca15c5dc80c2e0, 2026-02-27) frames PEA in the MCAS stack as oral; he separately specifies "Use ultra-micronized form for absorption," implying oral. No injectable PEA route is on record.
